description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a cream-colored fuselage and wings, adorned with red star insignias and the number '01' painted in blue on both the wings and tail. The airplane features two main wings stacked vertically, connected by struts, and a single wooden propeller at the nose. The cockpit is positioned just behind the propeller and appears to be open, typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The landing gear consists of two simple wheels mounted under the lower wing, with no visible nose wheel. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, each marked with a red star. The overall construction and finish suggest it is made primarily of wood, with a matte, slightly rough surface.
description: A small, cream-colored vintage biplane with red star markings, blue '01' numbers, and a wooden propeller. Features an open cockpit, simple fixed landing gear, and a classic tail design.
description_view_0: Oblique view showing the left side, upper wing, and tail with visible red stars and '01' markings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wooden propeller, open cockpit, and both main wings with red stars and '01' numbers.
description_view_2: Oblique view from the right rear, showing the tail, right wing, and propeller, with clear visibility of the '01' on the tail.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, both red stars on the wings, and the tail's red star.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the propeller; appears to be single-seat with no visible canopy.
